beats.text: He rips his vape. The cloud smells like a pancake factory on fire.

close.text: You nod. He nods. Neither of you says anything else. This is a language.

beats.text: He's staring at you. Not at your face. AT you. Sniffing, almost. His nostrils flare the way a cat's do when it smells a dying bird.

beats.speaker: Quikstop Teen
beats.text: Nah, nah, chill. I'm sayin' like — RESPECT. You chiefed today. I can smell it on the khakis.

beats.speaker: Quikstop Teen
beats.text: Got some STICKY in the alley if you wanna go another round. My guy cuts it with something. It hits different.

Your hand is already itching. The high you had earlier is fraying at the edges. You know this feeling. It's the jonesing feeling. The one that talks in your own voice and says things like 'just a little bit more, it's Saturday.'

choices.text: "I am a GROWN MAN I do NOT — " [walk away]

beats.speaker: Quikstop Teen
beats.text: BBQ. Yeah. So you WILL be smokin' burgers, like — conceptually, it's the same.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: That's not — that's not the same —
But you can already smell it through the baggie in his hoodie. Dank. Piney. Expensive. Your mouth fills with saliva like you're looking at a steak. This is embarrassing. This is who you are.

beats.speaker: Quikstop Teen
beats.text: My name's not Tyler. But aight.

to_alley.text: He's already walking. You follow a man-child toward a dumpster, telling yourself this is research. For the novel you are not writing.

nvm.text: You walk away. He watches you go. Somewhere in your chest, a small part of you is genuinely proud. The rest of you is SEETHING.

beats.text: He gives you a knowing nod. You are, somehow, in a club now.
